title: arenaria tetraquetra subsp. tomentosa
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra subsp. tomentosa, also known as woolly sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of woolly sandwort include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal medicine, woolly sandwort is often prepared as a tincture or infusement, and has been used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
